title: Vincetoxicum subramanii
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum subramanii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to the Western Ghats region of India. It is part of the genus Vincetoxicum, which is a group of milkweeds. The exact classification may be further specified as Vincetoxicum subramanii is a species of milkweed. It is a rare and endangered species, which contributes to its limited classification knowledge.

intro: Vincetoxicum subramanii, also known as Subramaniam's periploca, is a species of flowering plant in the dogbane family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Vincetoxicum subramanii include vincetoxicine, periplocin, and periplogenin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or extract, and is often used to make herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and teas.
